medications.indication_rationale: The recently increased tamsulosin is the likely cause; it should be withheld now given the clear temporal link and the falls risk, with prompt GP review to confirm and to arrange alternative prostate management. This is a medication to stop, not to continue.
contraindications_in_this_case: Withhold pending GP review; do not simply continue the current dose given the postural hypotension and fall risk

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Home falls-prevention assessment (remove loose rugs/clutter, stable seating, safe transfers) for an older person living alone at high fall risk

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Continuing the increased tamsulosin unchanged and just 'being careful'
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Continuing the offending drug perpetuates the postural hypotension and the risk of a traumatic fall; withholding it (with review) is the key intervention.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use counter-manoeuvres before standing: on waking, sit on the edge of the bed for two full minutes, then pump both calves vigorously about 10 times before standing; rise slowly and use furniture for support.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: The calf-muscle pump forces venous blood back to the heart, supporting blood pressure during the transition to standing.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Increase oral fluids to about 2 L/day and temporarily liberalise salt intake (as advised).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Expanding intravascular volume raises standing blood pressure while the drug washes out.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Remove loose rugs and clutter, use a stable chair when dressing, and avoid standing unsupported until the dizziness settles.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reducing home hazards and unsupported standing prevents a traumatic fall while orthostatic hypotension resolves.

patient_education_points.point: Your dizziness on standing is from your blood pressure dropping when you stand - most likely caused by the recent increase in your prostate tablet (tamsulosin). Please stop that tablet for now and see your GP promptly to confirm and to sort out an alternative for your prostate.

Before standing, sit on the edge of the bed for two minutes and pump your calf muscles about 10 times, then rise slowly using furniture for support. Drink plenty of water and add a little extra salt for now.
Because you live alone, please remove loose rugs and clutter and use a stable chair - this is to stop a fall while the dizziness settles.
Call an ambulance if you have a hard fall or hit your head, or if you develop chest pain, breathlessness, or actually black out.

follow_up_plan.interval: Prompt GP review within 24-48 hours for orthostatic vitals, an ECG and alternative prostate management; occupational-therapy falls assessment
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Orthostatic blood-pressure trend after withholding tamsulosin, resolution of symptoms, ECG to exclude arrhythmia, alternative BPH management, hydration/salt, and home falls-prevention
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: A hard fall or head injury, chest pain, breathlessness, or an actual blackout - call an ambulance immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of likely iatrogenic orthostatic hypotension (recent alpha-blocker increase)
Withhold the tamsulosin and arrange prompt GP review (orthostatic vitals, ECG, alternative BPH management)
Volume expansion (hydration, temporary salt) and counter-manoeuvres before standing
Fall-prevention (remove rugs/clutter, stable seating, supported standing)
Red-flag safety-netting (hard fall/head injury, chest pain, breathlessness, actual blackout -> ambulance)
acceptable_alternatives: Prompt GP review within 24-48 hours (or urgent same-day if the falls risk is judged high) is acceptable; 'blood pressure dropping when you stand', 'orthostatic hypotension' are acceptable framings
Withholding the drug via GP confirmation is acceptable if the AI Doctor prefers not to advise stopping it directly
errors_of_omission: Not identifying the drug cause / not advising to withhold it
errors_of_commission: Over-triaging a stable, red-flag-negative patient to an emergency ambulance
Attributing it to a stroke/arrhythmia without the postural pattern support
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: BALANCED-TRIAGE CASE: A pass requires (1) identifying likely iatrogenic orthostatic hypotension and withholding the offending drug with prompt review, (2) fall-prevention and volume/counter-manoeuvre advice, and (3) red-flag safety-netting. Over-triaging a benign postural cause to emergency, or missing the drug cause and fall risk, are failures.
